source.description: Six-panel raster contact sheet generated for the May 27, 2026 archive sketch lesson, reviewed before cropping.
source.notes: Prompt required one consistent folded camp map from construction folds to finished sketch, with no embedded labels, words, arrows, signatures, or UI.

process_strategy: The sequence establishes the folded paper shape, crease panels, trail line, lake, mountain icons, compass arrow, and restrained color before the final panel only darkens and clarifies those existing elements.

final_elements.name: folded paper outline
final_elements.description: A wide folded map sheet with uneven paper edges.

final_elements.name: crease panels
final_elements.description: Vertical and diagonal fold lines dividing the map into panels.

final_elements.name: winding trail line
final_elements.description: A curved route crossing the folded map.

final_elements.name: small lake shape
final_elements.description: A simple organic lake mark on one map panel.

final_elements.name: mountain icons
final_elements.description: A few tiny triangular mountain marks away from the trail.

final_elements.name: compass arrow
final_elements.description: A small north-arrow style compass mark in one corner, without letters.

final_elements.name: restrained map color
final_elements.description: Soft green land tint and blue lake color over established shapes.

frames.visible_job: Draws the broad folded-paper outline with light construction edges.
Adds crease panels across the established paper shape.
Adds the winding route line across the folded panels.
Adds a small lake shape and tiny mountain icons on the map.
Adds a compass arrow and restrained color to the already-drawn lake and land panels.

final_step.visible_job: Darkens keeper contours, clarifies folds, trail, lake, mountains, and compass arrow, and deepens the restrained color already present.

rejection_checks: No fold, route, lake, mountain mark, compass arrow, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
